ocac.school: Overseas Chinese Academy of Concord Suzhou (OCAC)
ocac.title: Scaling the Turnkey ASA Across Middle Grades
ocac.summary: OCAC established itself as a regional leader in neurotechnology by deploying the 16-week Turnkey ASA to large middle school cohorts. The program proved that BCI can scale across younger grades, driving institutional FOMO across Suzhou's top-tier international schools.

hangzhouNo2.title: The Global Neuroscience Pathway
hangzhouNo2.subtitle: How Hangzhou No.2 High School Qianjiang Connected Year 12 Students to the World's Leading Brain-Computer Interface Researchers
hangzhouNo2.challengeTitle: The Challenge: Standing Out in the Global Applicant Pool
hangzhouNo2.challengeContent: For the elite Year 12 students at the international division of Hangzhou No.2 High School Qianjiang, standard academic excellence is a given. However, as these students prepared their university applications for highly competitive neuroscience, pre-med, and engineering programs in China and abroad, they faced a critical hurdle: How do you prove genuine research capability and international collaboration before even entering university? The school administration needed a program that moved beyond standard robotics to offer authentic, cross-border academic networking.
hangzhouNo2.phase1Title: Phase 1: The Catalyst & Global Collaboration (April – May 2025)
hangzhouNo2.phase1P1: The journey began in April 2025 with a NeuroTX "Neuro-Discovery" Workshop. In just two days, students were introduced to the fundamentals of Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI), visualizing their own EEG signals and understanding the 50/50 Input/Output paradigm.
hangzhouNo2.phase1P2: The engagement was so profound that these Year 12 students immediately formed teams to enter the May 2025 br41n.io Global Hackathon.
hangzhouNo2.phase1Bullet: The Breakthrough: Because the hackathon is a global, virtual-hybrid event, the Hangzhou No.2 High School students were placed in collaborative projects alongside international teams. They spent 48 hours architecting algorithms and troubleshooting Python code with peers and mentors from across the globe, dealing with real clinical data and live BCI hardware.
hangzhouNo2.phase2Italic: Bridging the gap between the classroom and the research lab.
hangzhouNo2.phase2P1: Leveraging their school's international mobility program and their new status as br41n.io competitors, the students traveled to Europe in the summer of 2025. Because of their connection through NeuroTX, this wasn't just a standard study tour.
hangzhouNo2.phase2P2: The students physically visited official Hosting Institutes of the international BCI hackathon across Europe.
phase2List: They stepped inside world-class neuroscience labs.
They saw advanced research-grade hardware in action.
Most importantly, they stood face-to-face with the very researchers and professors who had judged their hackathon projects weeks earlier.
hangzhouNo2.phase3Title: Phase 3: The Deep Dive & Career Acceleration (Current: 2025 – 2026)
hangzhouNo2.phase3P1: Returning to Hangzhou transformed, these students recognized that BCI was their definitive career path. They enrolled in ongoing, advanced NeuroTX BCI lessons to master signal acquisition, artifact filtering, and machine learning classifiers.
hangzhouNo2.phase3P2: The Objective: The students are currently undertaking rigorous preparation for the upcoming April 2026 br41n.io Spring School and Hackathon.
phase3List: Rather than entering as beginners, they are now entering as veteran competitors.
They are utilizing the NeuroTX LMS and AI-assisted coding platforms to design highly advanced projects.

Their ultimate goal is to use the 2026 event to directly network with specific university professors to secure admissions and undergraduate research placements in top-tier neuroscience programs in both China and abroad.
hangzhouNo2.resultTitle: The Result: Unmatched ROI for the School and the Students
hangzhouNo2.resultP: Through the NeuroTX pipeline, Hangzhou No.2 High School Qianjiang provided its students with a priceless academic asset:
resultList: Verifiable International Collaboration: Students possess documented proof of working on global tech teams to solve real-world neuro-medical problems.
Direct Academic Networking: By bridging the virtual hackathon with physical European lab visits, the school facilitated direct networking between its high schoolers and elite university faculty.
Definitive Career Trajectories: The program solidified the students' ambitions, giving them a massive competitive edge for university admissions in highly selective STEM and neuroscience tracks.
hangzhouNo2.ctaQuestion: Does your school have ambitious students aiming for the world's top STEM universities?
hangzhouNo2.ctaP: NeuroTX provides the exact curriculum, hardware, and global network needed to get them there. Contact David Wiehls today to establish your school's pipeline to the international br41n.io network.

wenling.title: Bridging Local Excellence with Global Neuroscience
wenling.subtitle: How Zhejiang Wenling High School Catapulted Its Top Students into International BCI Research Networks
wenling.challengeTitle: The Challenge: Elevating Public School STEM to the Global Stage
wenling.challengeContent: Zhejiang Wenling High School is renowned for its rigorous academic standards and exceptional student talent. However, for their most ambitious senior students aiming to pursue elite careers in neuroscience, medicine, and advanced AI, traditional high school physics and computer science classes were no longer enough. To compete with applicants from elite international academies for top university spots—both within China and abroad—these students needed verifiable, hands-on experience in cutting-edge research and cross-border academic collaboration.
wenling.phase1Title: Phase 1: The Spark and the Sprint (April – May 2025)
wenling.phase1P1: In April 2025, NeuroTX introduced a specialized Tier 1 "Neuro-Discovery" Workshop to a select cohort of senior students at Wenling. The introduction to the 50/50 Input/Output world of Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI) was an instant catalyst.
wenling.phase1P2: Driven by this new frontier, these students boldly enrolled in the May 2025 br41n.io Global Hackathon just weeks later.
wenling.phase1Bullet: Global Integration: Rather than competing in an isolated local bracket, the Wenling High School cohort was integrated into international project teams. They collaborated virtually with peers and university mentors from around the world, applying AI classifiers and Python logic to real-world BCI challenges using data from the Unicorn Hybrid Black headset.
wenling.phase2Italic: From virtual collaboration to physical immersion.
wenling.phase2P1: To capitalize on their hackathon momentum, the Wenling cohort participated in a specialized international academic mobility program in the summer of 2025. Traveling to Europe, they didn't just tour campuses—they visited the official Hosting Institutes of the international BCI hackathon.
wenling.phase2Bullet: The Academic ROI: Students stepped inside authentic, world-class neurotechnology laboratories. They interacted face-to-face with the esteemed European professors, researchers, and engineers who had evaluated their hackathon projects just months prior. This transformed a virtual competition into genuine, career-defining academic networking (Guanxi).
wenling.phase3Title: Phase 3: The Deep Dive & The 2026 Campaign (Current)
wenling.phase3P1: Returning to Zhejiang with a crystallized vision of their futures, these students fully committed to the NeuroTX advanced curriculum. They recognized that BCI was not just an extracurricular activity, but their definitive career pathway.
wenling.phase3P2: Current Objective: The Wenling cohort is currently immersed in intensive NeuroTX BCI lessons, mastering signal acquisition and machine learning architecture in preparation for the April 2026 br41n.io Spring School and Hackathon.
phase3List: Career Trajectory: Instead of entering as novices, they are returning to the global stage as experienced competitors. Their ultimate goal is to leverage this 2026 hackathon to solidify relationships with leading neuroscience researchers, securing vital recommendations and undergraduate research placements at top-tier universities in China and internationally.
wenling.resultTitle: The Result: A Paradigm Shift for Public High School STEM
wenling.resultP: Through the NeuroTX pipeline, Zhejiang Wenling High School successfully bridged the gap between local academic rigor and global scientific innovation:
resultList: Elite University Portfolios: Students now possess unmatched application portfolios featuring international tech collaboration, applied machine learning, and advanced neurobiology.
Global Academic Mobility: The program provided public school students with a direct, verifiable bridge to European research institutes and global university faculty.
Future-Proof Career Paths: The school successfully launched its top STEM talent into the highly exclusive pipeline of global neuroscience and neuro-engineering.
wenling.ctaQuestion: Is your high school ready to connect its top STEM talent to the world's leading neuroscience researchers?
wenling.ctaP: NeuroTX provides the hardware, the curriculum, and the international network. Contact David Wiehls today to establish your school's pipeline to the br41n.io global hackathon.

entel.title: The Sino-German Tech Bridge
entel.subtitle: How Hangzhou Entel Foreign Language School Connected 50+ Students to Germany's Elite Engineering Universities
entel.challengeTitle: The Challenge: Pairing Linguistic Excellence with "Hard" Tech
entel.challengeContent: Hangzhou Entel Foreign Language School consistently produces students with exceptional cross-cultural communication skills and international ambitions. Many of these students specifically target Germany for its world-renowned, tuition-free engineering and tech universities. However, German university admissions—especially for institutions like the TU9—are notoriously rigorous regarding technical foundations. Entel administrators realized that fluency in German and English wasn't enough; their students needed a highly advanced STEM portfolio featuring verifiable research and engineering experience to stand out to European admissions committees.
entel.phase1P1: To bridge this gap, Entel partnered with NeuroTX to introduce Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI) to the student body. The response was massive.
entel.phase1P2: Over 50 students enrolled in the initial NeuroTX workshops. Drawn by the intersection of biology, computer science, and international collaboration, these students rapidly transitioned from learning basic Python to visualizing their own brainwaves.
entel.phase1P3: Capitalizing on this momentum, a large cohort of these students officially entered the 2025 br41n.io Global Hackathon. They worked on international teams, collaborating virtually to solve complex neuro-data challenges—proving to future universities that they could operate in a high-stakes, cross-border technical environment.
entel.phase2Title: Phase 2: The Direct Pipeline to Germany's Top Institutes
entel.phase2Italic: This is where the NeuroTX network fundamentally changed the students' trajectories.
entel.phase2P1: Because NeuroTX has deep European roots and is deeply integrated into the br41n.io hackathon network, the Entel students were granted exclusive networking access to some of Germany's most prestigious institutions.
entel.phase2P2: Through their hackathon participation and NeuroTX's partner network, the students directly connected with researchers, mentors, and official hosting institutes associated with:
phase2List: RWTH Aachen University (One of Europe's leading technical universities and the alma mater of NeuroTX's founder)
entel.phase3P1: The 2025 hackathon was just the beginning. Realizing the immense value of these European academic connections, the Entel students have continued their advanced BCI coursework with NeuroTX throughout the academic year.
entel.phase3P2: The Objective: These students are currently in intensive training for the upcoming April 2026 br41n.io Spring School and Hackathon. Their goal is to use their advanced BCI projects—ranging from medical data analysis to robotic control—as the centerpiece of their application portfolios for TUM, RWTH Aachen, and KIT, leveraging the relationships they have built over the past year to secure admissions into Germany's elite STEM programs.
entel.resultTitle: The Result: The Ultimate International Applicant
entel.resultP: Through the NeuroTX program, Hangzhou Entel Foreign Language School provided its students with a critical competitive advantage:
resultList: The "Linguist-Engineer" Profile: Students successfully combined their foreign language fluency with applied machine learning and neuroscience, creating a uniquely powerful university applicant profile.
Direct European Networking: The school bypassed traditional, generic study-abroad agencies and connected students directly to researchers at Germany's top technical institutes.

High-Volume Engagement: Scaling the program to 50+ students proved that advanced neurotechnology can be successfully deployed to large cohorts, driving massive value for the school's international department.
entel.ctaQuestion: Does your international school want to build a direct bridge to Europe's top technical universities?
entel.ctaP: NeuroTX provides the curriculum, the hardware, and the exclusive European network. Contact David Wiehls today to establish your school's pipeline to the br41n.io global hackathon.
